Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

In the Year of 1993, Jalal Abad State University came to existence and has since triumphed as one of the top universities in the country of Kyrgyzstan.

  • University In Kyrgyzstan

# Kyrgyzstan

Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

In the Year of 1993, Jalal Abad State University came to existence and has since triumphed as one of the top universities in the country of Kyrgyzstan. The University offers a myriad of programs such as medicine, engineering and even various natural sciences. It is famous for its highly rated education system which largely prioritizes research – this alone excels the institution’s reputation further to the international spectrum. Students from various countries flock towards the institution where quality education is delivered at affordable rates. It’s also has a friendly environment that helps international students ease into a new culture.

JASU degrees are recognized all around the globe, especially by international organizations such as the World Health Organization. Additionally, JASU also provides students with hostels while ensuring their safety and comfort. JASU is also located in a beautifiul region enriched with nature’s wonders while maintains a strong sense of community. All of this results in highly enjoyable educational and personal experiences.

Quick Highlights About Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

Year of Establishment1993
RecognitionNMC and WHO approved
EligibilityStudents should get 50% aggregate in PCB(Physics, Chemistry and Biology)
Course Duration6 Years
NEETQualifying Marks
IELTS/TOFELNot Required
Medium of TeachingEnglish

Why Choose Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan?

If you intend to study overseas, then JASU (Jalal Abad State University) is a great choice for a number of reasons. Let’s see them one by one.

High-Quality Education

The university has a reputable faculty of medicine, engineering, and natural sciences.

As part of the faculty, he is also an active researcher, and his teaching skills are well recognized.

Global Recognition

JASU is included in the listings of such organizations as the World Health Organization and many others.

This illustrates that your qualifications will be respected wherever you decide to pursue your career.

Affordable Tuition Fees

While the majority of other institutions impose high fees, JASU students get education at lower costs.

Friendly and Supportive Environment

JASU is open to international students and ensures a healthy, safe, and accommodating environment for them.

The facilities at the university campus are up to date, and the community is very helpful.

Beautiful Location

Besides, the university is located in a quiet place which contributes to effective studying and self development.

Jalal Abad State University is a great option for anyone looking to get quality education while paying affordable rates. The global recognition of JASU, the friendly surroundings and its stunning location all help students accomplish their academic and career objectives.

Fee Structure of Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

Particulars1st Year2nd Year3rd year4th Year5th Year6th Year
Tuition Fess4500 USD4500 USD4500 USD4500 USD4500 USD4500 USD
Hostel Fees600 USD600 USD600 USD600 USD600 USD600 USD

Here is the detailed fee structure planned for Jalal Abad State University (JASU) for the MBBS program :

First Year: $2,950

This is the first cost incurred in the MBBS program covering your tuition and some essential materials during the first year of the course.

Second Year : $2,950

Tuition charge remains the same in the second year ensuring that the fee structure is uniform throughout the academic term.

Third Year: $2,950

A year further, students pay the same amount while focusing their energy on academics without the stress of fees increasing.

Fourth Year: $2,950

The fee for the fourth year remains constant to allow efficient financial planning over the entire duration of the course.

Fifth Year: $2,950

Alongside the other years, tuition for the fifth year is also set at 2,950, providing an easier financial foundation for the majority of the course.

Sixth Year: $3,500

The fee increases marginally in the final year to $3,500 which caters for an additional offer of resources and training at the end of the academic program.

1 USD = 83.37 INR (approx.)

This rate assists exchange students in estimating their fees when they become residents of the new country.

Extra expenses:

In as much as the tuition cost is reasonable, other expenses like housing, meals, travel, and books need to be taken into account.

In totality, the fee structure at JASU is reasonable in comparison to most other medical universities worldwide, considering the standard of education provided.

Eligibility Criteria of Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

There are specific competency requirements for mela, othering and zone of proximal development criteria requirements that allow pupils to study at Jalal Abad State University (JASU).

Age Requirements.

Every candidate must attain the minimum age of 17 on or before December 31st in the year of intended enrollment into the university.

Educational Qualification.

Students must have a secondary school certificate (equivalent 10+2) with a minimum of 60% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (PCB).

Minimum Marks Set.

A minimum of 65% marks in PCB subjects (Physics, Chemistry, Biology) and at least 5Bs in O level or equivalents in Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Mathematics and one Other Subject is required.

Valid Passport.

All candidates must hold a valid passport with at least six months of validity remaining prior to the date of admission.

These are the most critical core requirements for the medicine (MBBS) program application at JASU. Ensure these conditions are set prior to submission.

Hostel Facilities At Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

Students studying at Jalal Abad State University (JASU) can benefit from modern amenities that are comfortable. Students can expect:

Security Arrangement.

Students residing in hostels are provided with round the clock security, which ensures each students safety is taken care of.

Accessibility of all Wi-Fi.

Students have access to high-speed Wi-Fi making it easy to study and talk to friends and family.

Proximity to Main Town:

The hostels are situated a short distance away from the university, enabling the students to walk to their classes and other facilities on the campus.

Social Facilities:

Students can freely interact and engage in recreational activities within the amenities. This fosters a good community among students.

Room Options:

The hostels have different types of rooms which are accommodating to students’ needs, including single and double occupancy rooms.

Students are guaranteed a comfortable stay and an environment conducive to studying through these amenities.

Student Life At Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

Life as a student at Jalal Abad State University (JASU) is productive, self-developing, and colorful. Here is a summary of what you can find there:

Academic Excellence:

JASU offers top notch education, particularly in health care, and places a high importance on education and on self-research. Students learn from reputable teachers and take part in meaningful research work.

International Students:

Over 70 different countries are represented at JASU which makes for a rich multicultural environment. You will meet and work together with fellow students from many cultural settings.

Sports Clubs:

JASU has sports clubs in basketball, football, swimming, badminton, and others for students who want to compete or have fun exercising. Students also participate in intercollegiate competitions.

Arts and culture clubs.

Students can join music, dance or drama clubs and showcase their creativity in front of their peers and many other students. These clubs also conduct events, performances and workshops for students.

Volunteering

JASU promotes social responsibility among students. The International Medical Students Association (IMSA) assists students to participate in social activities and enhances the welfare of the local community.

Supportive Campus Environment

The university has a friendly environment which helps international students to adjust easily. There is always someone willing to help students, and there are mentorship opportunities which help with professional and personal development.

In conclusion, life of a student at JASU is full of studies, sports, culture, and self-development, which are all important stages in a student’s life and study in terms of having rich experiences in life.

Ranking of Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

Jalal Abad State University (JASU) also has great reputation not only in Kyrgyzstan but also in other countries. Its rankings are as follows:

Country Rank: 22 in Kyrgyzstan

World Rank: 11,398 globally

These rankings speak for themselves and have marked the university’s efforts to gain a strong position as an academic institution in Kyrgyzstan, especially in regard to international healthcare education.

Advantages of Studying At Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

Study at Jalal Abad State University has many merits. Here are some of them.

Excellent academic performance:

JASU holds a strong reputation for imposing its students with high academic discipline in Medicine and other healthcare related courses. Students suggest JASU for its comprehensive curriculum and knowledgeable instructors.

International Students:

Graduates with JASU degrees have greater employability as the World Health Organization (WHO) and other international bodies endorse their qualification documents.

Reasonable Tuition Fees:

Like many other universities, JASU has very affordable tuition which makes higher education more attainable for students from abroad.

Living Expenses:

Students spend considerably less money on accommodation, food and other necessities in Kyrgyzstan in comparison to many other regions.

Location:

Students wishing to study in calm and serene places will appreciate Jalal-Abad region boasting of mountains and other pieces of nature ideal for self-study and self-improvement. Additionally, the university is located in a district which is very quiet and serene.

Students from other countries:

Over 70 countries are covered by JASU and thus the students are able to mingle and study with new people which unfortunately is not possible in many other countries.

Nurturing Environment At Campus.

With their student mentorship schemes and various student organizations, JASU assists its students on both personal and academic levels. As a result, JASU has a very welcoming environment.

To Summarize, JASU stands out as an institution of choice for students who desire to pursue high standard education with reasonable fees in a picturesque and positive environment.

Placement At Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

For graduates, especially concerning the medical field, Jalal Abad State University (JASU) assures good placement opportunities. Here’s a summary of placement options:

Earning Potential in the Medical Sector:

Several graduates have the scope to work in various hospitals and clinics following the completion of their MBBS degree. The Jalal Abad State University assists students in securing jobs by providing them with appropriate career support as well as connecting them with health institutions.

Further Studies and Specialization:

Other than the above, JASU offers its graduates with opportunities to obtain further studies and medicine specialization. Students can continue their academic career at JASU or any other preferred institution around the world.

Opportunities for Citizens of India:

After graduating from JASU, Indian students can also look out for jobs back in India. Many of these graduates prefer to join hospitals and clinics as well as further their education in medicine.

JASU provides suitable support resources to students so that they are ready to embark on their respective professional careers, whether they need to work or study further.

Education Loan for Indian Students

Indian students looking to study in institutions outside their home country, like Jalal Abad State University (JASU), have the option of applying for an education loan. Here’s what you need to know:

Amount Needed:

Education loans can be availed for a number of expenses such as tuition fees, accommodation, and any other expense that needs to be incurred to enhance learning. A student’s financial needs alongside the course fee determine the loan amount.

Evidence Needed:

NEET Score: Medical students, particularly those taking MBBS courses, may be required to provide the NEET score.

University Fee: The applicant must possess an admission letter from JASU which shows a fee breakdown.

Passport: An active passport which can be used to apply for a visa and a loan is essential.

Proof of Income from Parents: Banks will verify the income of the student’s parents or guardians to determine if they are eligible for the loan.

Other Documents: Academic certificates along with address proof and identity proof may also be required.

Loans: Different Types

Collateral-Free Loan: Many banks offer unsecured loans or loans without collateral for amounts that are under ₹7.5 Lakhs.

Secured Loan: Provisions of collateral such as property or fixed deposits are essential for students seeking loans of over the value of ₹7.5 Lakhs.

Interest Charges and Payment Back:

Depending on the bank with which a student opens an education loan account, the repayment of the loan can vary from 9% to 15%.

Usually, repayment starts after completion of the course, within the moratorium period ( which is commonly between 6 months to 1 year post course completion).

How To Apply:

Students are able to apply for education loans at nationalized banks, private banks, and non-banking financial companies’ (NBFCs).

Some other banks also provide pre-approved loans to students already enrolled in recognized universities such as JASU.

Help From Educational Consultants:

At Bright Future Abroad Studies, the educational consultants assist the students with loan applications and offer advice on which loan is better suited.

This type of financial aid facilitates overseas study by easing financial burdens so students can concentrate on their education.

FAQs At Jalal Abad State University, Kyrgyzstan

The following is a set of questions that prospective students and willing learners might have about Jalal Abad State University (JASU) – the university and its offerings:

What programs are available at Jalal Abad State University?

The institution provides an extensive assortment of undergraduate and master’s programs, including courses in medicine, engineering, education, and natural sciences. JASU is famous for its MBBS program.

What intake procedure does JASU have?

Students eager to enroll should ensure they meet the eligibility criteria. The following should include a passport, a minimum of 60% in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (PCB) subjects in secondary education, and age minimum of 17. The application process includes submitting documentation such as certificates of qualifications, and a passport.

How much money do I need in order to study at JASU?

The first five years of studying MBBS starts at $2950 per year and increases to $3500 for the final year of study. Apart from these figures are accommodation and other living expenses.

Are there Scholarships offered by JASU?

Compared to other international institutions, JASU offers relatively inexpensive tuition fees. A particular scholarship might be available, but most students fund their expenses through education loans or their family’s finances.

Describe student life at JASU?

More than 70 nationalities means student life at JASU is rich and colorful. The university has sports clubs, arts and culture clubs, and community service clubs to ensure students are able to participate in activities beyond the classroom.

This is a summary of questions and answers which highlight important details pertaining to studying at Jalal Abad State University.
